column1: # Trial: GP 1: Virat CC vs Dallas Cyclones - Qualifier 2 - GPCC Cup 2025

column3: GP 1: Virat CC vs Dallas Cyclones - Qualifier 2 - GPCC Cup 2025 | Played on: 2025-02-01 at Grand Prairie Cricket Ground

We like to thank: | All Stars Cricket Academy for volunteering to make sample bail safety devices for trials | Grand Prairie Cricket Club (GPCC) for conducting the tournament + allowing this trial in a live match + great camera footage | Umpires and players of the 2 teams for their participation and valuable feedback on behavior of the device during the match

This game featured better design and materials for the device | 1. Silicone collar | 2. Stronger thread | 3. No O-ring to connect bails to thread. Thread knotted to bail directly using Scaffold knot with 4 wraps. Was much stronger during impact testing.

Next video shows trial of an alternate design | | Collar repositioned to rest on stopper instead of being below stopper | | Benefit(s): | No collar weight (however little) acting on bail as collar rests on stopper | | Drawback(s): | 1. No blocking stopper. In extreme case; collar could lift beyond stump without stopper blocking upward movement | 2. If resting stopper (and collar) are too high; tether thread no longer taut; can sway in wind. Keeper's gloves or bat can touch the swaying thread | | Please see: https://zenodo.org/records/18043789 Supplemental Technical FAQ PDF on this alternate design (which is not chosen)
Not related to bail safety data collection | (but cannot miss this lovely catch by Karthick Manoharan)
